repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->
Fixes #38234

Problem:
Under certain circumstances (e.g. using gzipped manpages with mandoc),
:Man will not find the correct page because it does not process
multiple extensions correctly.
For example, with a file named strcpy.3p.gz, it will only check the .gz
part to try to check the section.
This leads to some pages being inaccessible because it will return the
page from the wrong section.

Solution:
A loop which tests multiple extensions to try to find one which matches
the name of the section.
Also refactored the man.get_path function so that it can be tested.

This PR also adds tests for a few different cases like this.

comment_data.body: is there a way to avoid reaching into the internals? it makes the tests very brittle and ties us to a specific implementation. other tests in this file show possible approaches, though they admittedly also use similar hacks (but would like to avoid accumulating more)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -259,6 +259,59 @@ describe(':Man', function()
     os.remove(actual_file)
   end)
 
+  -- if man -w returns multiple results, :Man should select the correct one.
+  it('matches correct manpage section and name', function()
+    -- mock the system function to return the results we want to test.
+    local function doTest(results, name, sect)
+      return exec_lua(function()
+        local man = require 'man'
+
+        --- @diagnostic disable-next-line:duplicate-set-field
+        man._system = function()

comment_data.body: this can stay `local` , it's not used in the test?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -216,7 +216,7 @@ end
 
 --- @param name? string
 --- @param sect? string
-local function get_path(name, sect)
+function M._get_path(name, sect)

comment_data.body: usually a good practice to choose a fixed limit (`for i = 1, 3 do`) to avoid infinite loops, in case of bugs.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -270,7 +270,18 @@ local function get_path(name, sect)
   if #namematches > 0 and sect ~= '' then
     --- @param v string
     sectmatches = vim.tbl_filter(function(v)
-      return fn.fnamemodify(v, ':e') == sect
+      -- On some systems, there are multiple extensions, e.g. strlen.3.gz
+      -- We must test all of the extensions to make sure we get the correct match.
+      local root = v
+      while true do

@@ -259,6 +259,59 @@ describe(':Man', function()
     os.remove(actual_file)
   end)
 
+  -- if man -w returns multiple results, :Man should select the correct one.
+  it('matches correct manpage section and name', function()
+    -- mock the system function to return the results we want to test.
+    local function doTest(results, name, sect)

comment_data.body:
Hi, thank you for the review.
I was trying to match the approach of the other tests, i.e. line 25 where _find_path is mocked. Your criticism is fair though.

I'll try refactoring the get_path function instead to avoid having to mock _system.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -259,6 +259,59 @@ describe(':Man', function()
     os.remove(actual_file)
   end)
 
+  -- if man -w returns multiple results, :Man should select the correct one.
+  it('matches correct manpage section and name', function()
+    -- mock the system function to return the results we want to test.
+    local function doTest(results, name, sect)
+      return exec_lua(function()
+        local man = require 'man'
+
+        --- @diagnostic disable-next-line:duplicate-set-field
+        man._system = function()

@@ -259,6 +259,59 @@ describe(':Man', function()
     os.remove(actual_file)
   end)
 
+  -- if man -w returns multiple results, :Man should select the correct one.
+  it('matches correct manpage section and name', function()
+    -- mock the system function to return the results we want to test.
+    local function doTest(results, name, sect)

@@ -270,7 +270,18 @@ local function get_path(name, sect)
   if #namematches > 0 and sect ~= '' then
     --- @param v string
     sectmatches = vim.tbl_filter(function(v)
-      return fn.fnamemodify(v, ':e') == sect
+      -- On some systems, there are multiple extensions, e.g. strlen.3.gz
+      -- We must test all of the extensions to make sure we get the correct match.
+      local root = v
+      while true do

comment_data.body: I've refactored so that the get_path and system functions are still local - separated the relevant part into M._match_manpage_path
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -216,7 +216,7 @@ end
 
 --- @param name? string
 --- @param sect? string
-local function get_path(name, sect)
+function M._get_path(name, sect)

comment_data.body: I've refactored this so that the system and get_path functions are still local, and separated the relevant part to M._match_manpage_path
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -259,6 +259,59 @@ describe(':Man', function()
     os.remove(actual_file)
   end)
 
+  -- if man -w returns multiple results, :Man should select the correct one.
+  it('matches correct manpage section and name', function()
+    -- mock the system function to return the results we want to test.
+    local function doTest(results, name, sect)
+      return exec_lua(function()
+        local man = require 'man'
+
+        --- @diagnostic disable-next-line:duplicate-set-field
+        man._system = function()

comment_regular_data.body: Thanks. Please clearly, but concisely, explain the problem and include it also in the commit message.
Thank you for the review! I think I've made all the changes as requested now, and it's ready for re-review.

title: fix(man.lua): :Man ignores section of gzipped manpage
